# Deploy ForgeFlowKit to forgeflowkit.com (Tencent Registrar + Cloudflare DNS-only + Pages) Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Take ForgeFlowKit from "built locally" to "live at forgeflowkit.com (overseas, no ICP filing) with privacy-friendly analytics" — enabling real-world SEO and traffic data.

**Architecture:** Static site generated by Astro 4.16.19 (141 HTML pages). Deployed to **Cloudflare Pages** (free global CDN, free SSL). Domain `forgeflowkit.com` registered at **Tencent Cloud**; DNS moved to **Cloudflare DNS-only zone** (free; Cloudflare proxy disabled — used only as authoritative DNS) so Cloudflare Pages can auto-manage apex CNAME flattening. Plausible Analytics for GDPR-friendly tracking. Gitee (primary) → GitHub mirror.

**Tech Stack:** Astro 4.16.19 (static) · Cloudflare Pages (hosting + CDN) · Tencent Cloud Registrar (domain) · Cloudflare DNS (free DNS-only zone) · DNSPod (NS switch) · Plausible Analytics · Gitee → GitHub mirror · @astrojs/sitemap

**Decisions changed from plan-v1 (2026-06-22):**

| Aspect | plan-v1 | plan-v2 (this) | Why |
|---|---|---|---|
| Domain registrar | Cloudflare Registrar | **Tencent Cloud** | User purchased on Tencent (2026-06-23) |
| DNS provider | Cloudflare (proxy) | **Cloudflare (DNS only)** | Need apex CNAME flattening for Pages; only Cloudflare DNS supports this |
| Deploy region | Unspecified | **Overseas** | No ICP filing required |
| Pages integration | Auto-DNS | Auto-DNS (after NS switch) | Same end state, longer setup |

**Pre-flight verified 2026-06-22 (carried over):**
- ✅ `astro.config.mjs` has `site: 'https://forgeflowkit.com'` (line 6)
- ✅ `src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro` exists with `<head>` section
- ✅ `@astrojs/sitemap` integration configured
- ✅ `pnpm build` produces 141 pages
- ✅ Git remote: `gitee.com:wlz679/calcKit.git` only (no GitHub yet)
- ✅ Master branch

**⚠️ Working-tree warning (2026-06-23):**
Working tree has **unrelated in-flight changes** from a separate i18n drift fix:
- Modified: `dist/en/solopreneur-burn-rate-calculator/index.html`
- Modified: `dist/zh/solopreneur-burn-rate-calculator/index.html`
- Modified: `src/i18n/translations.ts`
- Untracked: `scripts/_audit-howTouse.cjs`
- Untracked: `scripts/_verify-counts.cjs`

These must **NOT** be included in deploy commits. The deploy code work affects `src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro` and adds 2 new files in `public/` — none of which touch the above. **Recommended: do the deploy work in a fresh git worktree** (`superpowers:using-git-worktrees`) to avoid any conflict. If you must work on master, use explicit file paths in `git add` (never `git add -u dist/` or `git add .`).

## Implementation Status (2026-06-23) — Tasks 1-3 already done on master

**Pre-flight verification on 2026-06-23 reveals Tasks 1-3 were already implemented on master BEFORE this amended plan was created.** The original plan-v1 (`2026-06-22-deploy-forgeflowkit.md`) was written *after* the code was committed, and listed these as "to do" — a documentation/reality drift that future agents could otherwise re-execute and duplicate.

| Task | Status | Commit on master |
|---|---|---|
| 1. Plausible in BaseLayout | ✅ **DONE** | `53a01ea feat(analytics): add Plausible script to BaseLayout (deferred, 1KB, GDPR-friendly)` (2026-06-22 16:33) |
| 2. `public/_headers` (Cloudflare Pages) | ✅ **DONE** | `bdbc19b feat(deploy): add Cloudflare Pages _headers (security + cache)` |
| 3. `public/_redirects` (www→apex 301) | ✅ **DONE** | `838035b feat(deploy): add www→apex 301 redirect via Cloudflare Pages _redirects` |

Verification (run from repo root on master):

```bash
# BaseLayout has Plausible with correct data-domain
grep 'plausible.io' src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro

# _headers and _redirects exist in public/
ls public/_headers public/_redirects

# Plausible is in the built dist output (140 of 141 pages — 1 page
# likely uses a different layout or is a non-HTML asset like robots.txt)
grep -lr 'plausible.io' dist/ | wc -l   # expect: 140
```

**When executing this plan, skip Tasks 1-3 entirely.** The sections below are retained as historical reference (what was done) and as the canonical specification if anything ever needs to be re-verified or re-applied. Do **not** re-add Plausible / `_headers` / `_redirects` — they already exist.

**The only remaining work is Tasks 4-9** — all user-action (4-8) plus post-launch verification (9). The amended Task 4 and Task 7 below adapt the user-action steps for the **Tencent registrar + Cloudflare DNS-only** strategy.

## Task 1: Add Plausible analytics to BaseLayout

**Files:**
- Modify: `src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro` (insert after `<link rel="icon" ...>` line, before `{schema && ...}` line)

**Why:** Plausible gives GDPR-friendly traffic analytics without cookie banners. Single `<script>` tag, ~1 KB, no impact on Core Web Vitals.

- [ ] **Step 1.1: Read BaseLayout to locate insertion point**

Read `src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro` and find the `<link rel="icon" type="image/svg+xml" href="/favicon.svg" />` line. Confirm the line before `{schema && ...}` (the schema JSON-LD line) comes right after.

- [ ] **Step 1.2: Insert Plausible script**

Add immediately after the favicon link line (before the `{schema && ...}` line):

```astro
<script defer data-domain="forgeflowkit.com" src="https://plausible.io/js/script.js"></script>
```

Resulting `<head>` block should look like:

```astro
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" />
  <title>{title}</title>
  <meta name="description" content={description} />
  <meta property="og:title" content={title} />
  <meta property="og:description" content={description} />
  <meta property="og:image" content={ogImage} />
  <meta property="og:type" content="website" />
  <meta name="robots" content="index, follow" />
  <link rel="sitemap" href="/sitemap-index.xml" />
  <link rel="icon" type="image/svg+xml" href="/favicon.svg" />
  <script defer data-domain="forgeflowkit.com" src="https://plausible.io/js/script.js"></script>
  {schema && <script type="application/ld+json" set:html={schema} />}
</head>
```

- [ ] **Step 1.3: Build and verify the script tag is present in dist/**

Run: `pnpm build 2>&1 | tail -5`
Expected: "141 page(s) built" — build succeeds.

Then run: `grep -l "plausible.io/js/script.js" dist/en/solopreneur-cac-calculator/index.html`
Expected: prints the file path (Plausible script is in the rendered output).

Also verify it appears in multiple pages (sanity check that BaseLayout applies globally):
Run: `grep -lr "plausible.io/js/script.js" dist/en/ | wc -l`
Expected: ~33 (one per English page).

- [ ] **Step 1.4: Commit (selective — exclude unrelated working-tree files)**

```bash
git add src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro
git add dist/
# Verify staged changes are deploy-only — no i18n drift files, no burn-rate dist
git status
git diff --staged --stat | head -20
git commit -m "feat(analytics): add Plausible script to BaseLayout (deferred, 1KB, GDPR-friendly)"
```

**Sanity check before committing:** `git status` should show only BaseLayout + 141 dist files staged. If you see `solopreneur-burn-rate-calculator/index.html` or `src/i18n/translations.ts` staged, stop and unstage them: `git restore --staged <path>`.

---

## Task 2: Add Cloudflare Pages security headers

**Files:**
- Create: `public/_headers`

**Why:** `_headers` is a Cloudflare Pages convention (also works on Netlify). Lets us set security + cache headers without per-page config. Without this, the site ships with zero CSP / X-Frame-Options / etc.

- [ ] **Step 2.1: Create `public/_headers`**

Create file `public/_headers` with the following content (Unix LF line endings — no CRLF):

```
/*
  X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
  X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN
  Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin
  Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=()
  Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload
  # Astro already inlines critical CSS, so cache HTML aggressively
  Cache-Control: public, max-age=0, must-revalidate

/_astro/*
  # Astro fingerprint hashes content; cache aggressively (1 year)
  Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable

/sitemap-index.xml
  /sitemap-0.xml
  Cache-Control: public, max-age=3600

/favicon.svg
  /og-default.png
  Cache-Control: public, max-age=86400
```

The `_headers` syntax: path-glob on its own line, then indented header lines beneath it. Cloudflare Pages reads this from `public/` at deploy time.

- [ ] **Step 2.2: Build and verify _headers is copied to dist**

Run: `pnpm build 2>&1 | tail -3`
Then: `ls dist/_headers && echo "exists" && cat dist/_headers`
Expected: file exists in dist root with the content above.

- [ ] **Step 2.3: Commit**

```bash
git add public/_headers dist/_headers
git diff --staged --stat
git commit -m "feat(deploy): add Cloudflare Pages _headers (security + cache)"
```

---

## Task 3: Add www → apex redirect (canonical domain enforcement)

**Files:**
- Create: `public/_redirects`

**Why:** SEO best practice — pick one canonical domain (apex `forgeflowkit.com`) and 301 redirect the other (`www.forgeflowkit.com`). Prevents duplicate-content penalty.

- [ ] **Step 3.1: Create `public/_redirects`**

Create file `public/_redirects` with the following content (Unix LF):

```
# www → apex (301 permanent)
https://www.forgeflowkit.com/* https://forgeflowkit.com/:splat 301

# Force HTTPS (Cloudflare Pages does this automatically, but explicit is safer)
/http://* https://forgeflowkit.com/:splat 301
```

The first rule handles `www.forgeflowkit.com` → `forgeflowkit.com`. The second is a safety net for `http://` URLs.

- [ ] **Step 3.2: Build and verify _redirects is copied**

Run: `pnpm build 2>&1 | tail -3`
Then: `cat dist/_redirects`
Expected: shows both redirect rules.

- [ ] **Step 3.3: Commit**

```bash
git add public/_redirects dist/_redirects
git diff --staged --stat
git commit -m "feat(deploy): add www→apex 301 redirect via Cloudflare Pages _redirects"
```

---

## Task 4 (AMENDED): Buy domain via Tencent + move DNS to Cloudflare

> ⚠️ **USER ACTION** — requires Tencent Cloud account + Cloudflare account + payment method. Cannot be fully automated. (Domain already purchased on Tencent per 2026-06-23 — only DNS switch steps remain.)

**Files:** None (DNS config happens entirely in web UIs.)

- [ ] **Step 4.1: Create Cloudflare account (if not already)**

Go to https://dash.cloudflare.com/sign-up and sign up. **Free tier is sufficient** — we are only using Cloudflare's DNS service, not its proxy/CDN.

- [ ] **Step 4.2: Add forgeflowkit.com as Cloudflare zone (DNS only)**

1. In Cloudflare dashboard, click "Add a site" (or "Add" → "Connect a domain").
2. Enter `forgeflowkit.com` → click "Continue".
3. Select **Free plan** ($0/month).
4. Cloudflare will scan existing DNS records (~1 minute). Review the records — there shouldn't be any yet since the domain was just purchased.
5. **Critical configuration step:** On the records review page, ensure proxy status is **"DNS only"** (grey cloud icon ☁️, NOT orange 🟠) for any records. Since there are no records yet, this is moot — just click "Continue" / "Done".
6. Cloudflare will now display **two nameserver hostnames** that you must point your domain to. Copy them. They look like:
   ```
   anna.ns.cloudflare.com
   bob.ns.cloudflare.com
   ```
   (Yours will differ — Cloudflare assigns randomly.)

- [ ] **Step 4.3: Change NS records at Tencent DNSPod**

1. Go to https://console.cloud.tencent.com/domain
2. Find `forgeflowkit.com` in the domain list → click "解析" (Resolve / DNSPod).
3. In the domain detail page, navigate to "域名设置" (Domain settings) tab.
4. Find the "DNS 服务器" (DNS Servers) section.
5. Click "修改 DNS 服务器" (Modify DNS Servers) or "设置 DNS" depending on UI version.
6. Replace the default Tencent nameservers (e.g., `ns3.dnsv5.com`, `ns4.dnsv5.com`) with the **two Cloudflare nameservers from Step 4.2**.
7. Confirm. Tencent may require SMS verification.

- [ ] **Step 4.4: Wait for NS propagation (24-48 hours)**

DNS propagation takes 24-48 hours globally. During this window:
- The site may be intermittently unreachable if any DNS query hits an old (Tencent) nameserver.
- Some users (especially in China) may still see Tencent's nameservers until their ISP refreshes.
- This is **normal** — do not panic, do not change anything.

If you need to check progress:
- https://dnschecker.org/#NS/forgeflowkit.com — global propagation map
- Terminal: `dig NS forgeflowkit.com +short` (run from outside China for accurate results)

- [ ] **Step 4.5: Verify NS switched successfully**

Terminal:
```bash
dig NS forgeflowkit.com +short
```

Expected output: two lines starting with the Cloudflare nameserver hostnames from Step 4.2, e.g.:
```
anna.ns.cloudflare.com.
bob.ns.cloudflare.com.
```

If you see Tencent nameservers (e.g., `ns3.dnsv5.com`), propagation is not yet complete — wait longer.

Also verify in Cloudflare dashboard → `forgeflowkit.com` → Overview page. Status should show **"Active"** (not "Pending"). When Cloudflare detects the NS switch, it transitions from Pending → Active automatically (usually within minutes after global propagation reaches ~50%).

---

## Task 5: Create GitHub repo + configure Gitee mirror

> ⚠️ **USER ACTION** — requires GitHub account. Cloudflare Pages integrates with GitHub (not Gitee directly), so we mirror.

**Files:** None.

Cloudflare Pages free tier integrates with GitHub (or GitLab). Strategy: keep Gitee as primary remote, mirror to GitHub via Gitee's built-in mirror feature.

- [ ] **Step 5.1: Create empty GitHub repo**

1. Go to https://github.com/new
2. Repository name: `calcKit` (matches Gitee repo name)
3. Visibility: **Public** (Cloudflare Pages free tier requires public repo for static site deploys)
4. **Do NOT** initialize with README, .gitignore, or license (Gitee repo already has these — would cause merge conflicts)
5. Click "Create repository"

- [ ] **Step 5.2: Configure Gitee → GitHub mirror**

1. Go to https://gitee.com/wlz679/calcKit (existing Gitee repo)
2. Click "管理" (Management) → "仓库设置" (Repo settings) → "镜像管理" (Mirror management) — note: this feature requires Gitee Pro / 企业版; for free accounts, fall back to Step 5.3
3. Under "镜像到 GitHub", click "添加镜像" (Add mirror)
4. Paste the GitHub repo URL: `https://github.com/<your-username>/calcKit.git`
5. Authentication: provide GitHub personal access token with `repo` scope
6. Enable "Push mirror" (主动推送镜像)
7. Click "确定" (Confirm)

After this, every `git push gitee master` will automatically push to GitHub as well.

- [ ] **Step 5.3 (Fallback if mirror unavailable): Manual mirror**

```bash
git remote add github https://github.com/<your-username>/calcKit.git
git push github master
```

(One-time. For subsequent pushes, use `git push github master` manually, or configure `git config --global push.default matching` and add to script.)

- [ ] **Step 5.4: Verify mirror works**

After pushing new commits (e.g., after Task 3's commit):
- GitHub repo `https://github.com/<your-username>/calcKit` should have the latest commits.
- Mirror may take 1-2 minutes to propagate.

---

## Task 6: Connect Cloudflare Pages to GitHub repo

> ⚠️ **USER ACTION** — done in Cloudflare dashboard. Must complete **after Task 4** (DNS active on Cloudflare).

**Files:** None.

- [ ] **Step 6.1: Create Cloudflare Pages project**

1. In Cloudflare dashboard, click "Workers & Pages" → "Create" → "Pages" tab → "Connect to Git"
2. Select GitHub, authorize Cloudflare to access the repo (one-time OAuth).
3. Pick the `calcKit` repo.
4. Click "Begin setup"

- [ ] **Step 6.2: Configure build settings**

| Setting | Value |
|---|---|
| Project name | `calckit` (becomes `calckit.pages.dev` for the preview URL) |
| Production branch | `master` |
| Framework preset | `Astro` (Cloudflare auto-detects) |
| Build command | `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && pnpm build` |
| Build output directory | `dist` |
| Root directory | (leave empty) |
| Environment variables | `NODE_VERSION` = `20` |
| | `PNPM_VERSION` = `9` |

Why `--frozen-lockfile`: ensures Cloudflare's build matches the lockfile committed to the repo (prevents drift).

- [ ] **Step 6.3: Save and Deploy**

Click "Save and Deploy". First build takes 2-3 minutes. Cloudflare shows build logs in real-time.

Expected: "Success! Your site is deployed to https://calckit.pages.dev" (preview URL, not yet custom domain).

- [ ] **Step 6.4: Verify preview URL works**

Open `https://calckit.pages.dev` in a browser. Should show ForgeFlowKit landing page.
Open `https://calckit.pages.dev/sitemap-index.xml` — should show sitemap index.
Open `https://calckit.pages.dev/en/solopreneur-cac-calculator` — should show CAC calculator.

If any page fails, check the build log in Cloudflare dashboard. Most common issue: missing `NODE_VERSION` env var → Cloudflare uses old Node which doesn't support Astro 4.

---

## Task 7 (AMENDED): Add forgeflowkit.com as Cloudflare Pages custom domain

> ⚠️ **USER ACTION** — done in Cloudflare dashboard. Requires Task 4 NS propagation + Task 5/6 complete.

**Files:** None (DNS auto-managed since Cloudflare is now authoritative for the zone).

- [ ] **Step 7.1: Add custom domain to Pages project**

1. In Cloudflare Pages project dashboard, click "Custom domains" tab.
2. Click "Set up a custom domain"
3. Enter `forgeflowkit.com` → Click "Continue"
4. Cloudflare detects the domain is on Cloudflare DNS and offers to **auto-add** a CNAME record. The record will be:
   ```
   forgeflowkit.com  CNAME  calckit.pages.dev  (DNS only, grey cloud)
   ```
   Click "Activate domain". Cloudflare Pages will also auto-provision the SSL certificate via Let's Encrypt.

5. (Optional but recommended) Add `www.forgeflowkit.com` → Cloudflare auto-adds CNAME → the `_redirects` rule from Task 3 sends traffic to apex.

- [ ] **Step 7.2: Verify HTTPS works**

After 1-2 minutes (DNS propagation of new CNAME + SSL provisioning):
- Open `https://forgeflowkit.com` — should show ForgeFlowKit with valid SSL (no browser warning).
- Open `https://www.forgeflowkit.com` — should redirect (301) to `https://forgeflowkit.com`.
- Click the padlock icon → should show "Connection is secure" issued by Cloudflare / Let's Encrypt.

- [ ] **Step 7.3: Verify Plausible is tracking**

1. Open `https://forgeflowkit.com` in a browser
2. Open browser DevTools → Network tab → reload
3. Should see a request to `https://plausible.io/js/script.js` (200 OK)
4. Navigate to a calculator page (e.g., `/en/solopreneur-cac-calculator`) → should see another Plausible request (pageview)
5. Go to https://plausible.io/forgeflowkit.com (your Plausible dashboard) → wait 5-10 seconds → should show the pageviews

If Plausible shows nothing: clear browser cache, disable ad blockers (Plausible is sometimes blocked by privacy extensions).

---

## Task 8: Submit sitemap to Search Console + Bing

> ⚠️ **USER ACTION** — requires Google + Bing accounts. **Can run in parallel with Task 7** (DNS propagation doesn't affect sitemap submission; Google will retry indexing once DNS resolves).

**Files:** None (search console verification is web-UI only).

- [ ] **Step 8.1: Google Search Console**

1. Go to https://search.google.com/search-console/
2. Click "Add property" → "URL prefix" → enter `https://forgeflowkit.com`
3. Verification method: **DNS TXT record** (preferred — no code change)
   - Google provides a TXT record like `google-site-verification=abc123...`
   - In Cloudflare DNS for `forgeflowkit.com`, add a TXT record:
     - Name: `@`
     - Content: `<the Google verification string>`
     - TTL: Auto
   - Back in Search Console, click "Verify" — usually passes within 1-2 minutes.
4. After verification, go to "Sitemaps" (left sidebar) → enter `sitemap-index.xml` → click "Submit"
5. Wait 24-48 hours for Google to crawl. Check "Pages" report to see indexed pages.

- [ ] **Step 8.2: Bing Webmaster Tools**

1. Go to https://www.bing.com/webmasters
2. "Add a site" → enter `https://forgeflowkit.com`
3. Verification: same TXT record from Step 8.1 works for both — Bing verifies via the same record.
4. Submit sitemap: `https://forgeflowkit.com/sitemap-index.xml`

- [ ] **Step 8.3: Verify hreflang tags**

The site has en/zh pages. Verify the `hreflang` setup is correct:
- `curl -s https://forgeflowkit.com/en/solopreneur-cac-calculator | grep -i hreflang`
- Expected: shows `<link rel="alternate" hreflang="en" href="https://forgeflowkit.com/en/...">` and `<link rel="alternate" hreflang="zh" href="https://forgeflowkit.com/zh/...">`

If hreflang tags are missing, that's a separate Astro config issue — file a follow-up plan to add `@astrojs/i18n` integration. For now, en and zh are still indexable separately.

---

## Task 9: Lighthouse audit + fix critical issues

**Files:** Varies based on findings (likely no changes if pre-flight checks pass).

- [ ] **Step 9.1: Run Lighthouse against production URL**

Using Chrome DevTools:
1. Open `https://forgeflowkit.com/en/solopreneur-cac-calculator` (most representative page)
2. DevTools → Lighthouse tab
3. Select: Performance, Accessibility, Best Practices, SEO
4. Mode: Mobile
5. Click "Analyze page load"

Expected scores (targets):
- Performance: ≥ 90
- Accessibility: ≥ 95
- Best Practices: ≥ 95
- SEO: = 100 (we already have og:tags, meta description, sitemap, robots)

- [ ] **Step 9.2: Fix any issues found**

Common issues and fixes (if any):
- **Performance < 90**: Likely the bar chart in `ai-api-cost-comparison` or model list in `openai-token-calculator` shipping too much JS. May need code splitting. Out of scope for this plan — file a follow-up.
- **Accessibility < 95**: Likely color contrast on tier indicators (🟢🟡🟠🔴). May need text labels (🟢 Healthy). Out of scope — file follow-up.
- **Best Practices < 95**: Check console for errors. Common cause: missing `<meta name="theme-color">`. Easy fix in BaseLayout.
- **SEO < 100**: Almost certainly will be 100 since we have og:tags, structured data, sitemap, robots meta.

Document any < 90 score in a follow-up issue. Do NOT block deployment on this — ship and iterate.

**Scope check:** Single subsystem (deployment + analytics). Did not split into sub-plans.

**Out of scope (file as separate plans if needed):**
- Adding Plausible custom event tracking (button clicks, etc.) — can be added later
- A/B testing
- Adding new calculators (direction 1)
- V4 engine upgrade (direction 3)

**Working-tree isolation (recommended):** Use a fresh git worktree for Tasks 1-3 to avoid clobbering the in-flight i18n drift fix. See "Working-tree warning" above.

## Execution Handoff

Plan complete and saved to `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-23-deploy-forgeflowkit-tencent-dns.md`. Two execution options:

**1. Subagent-Driven (recommended)** — dispatch a fresh subagent per task, review between tasks, fast iteration. Good for parallelizing Tasks 1-3 (each is independent).

**2. Inline Execution** — execute tasks in this session using `executing-plans`, batch execution with checkpoints.

**Recommended for Tasks 1-3:** Use a fresh git worktree + inline execution. The worktree isolates from the in-flight i18n drift fixes in master.

**Note on user-action tasks:** Tasks 4-8 are USER ACTION (require Cloudflare account, GitHub account, Tencent DNSPod access, payment, dashboard clicks). The plan documents the steps but Claude cannot execute them. The user will do these manually while Claude handles Tasks 1-3 (code work) and Task 9 (verification post-launch).

**Critical-path timing for user:**
- Task 4 NS switch starts the 24-48h propagation clock
- Once propagation completes (~1 day), Tasks 5-7 can run in immediate sequence (~10 min total)
- Task 8 (sitemap submission) can run in parallel with Task 7

**Suggested sequence for the user:**
1. Start Task 4.2-4.3 immediately (Cloudflare zone add + NS switch) — this starts propagation
2. While waiting: do Task 5 (GitHub repo + mirror)
3. Once NS active (Task 4.5): do Task 6 (Pages connect to GitHub) → wait for first deploy
4. Do Task 7 (custom domain bind) — Cloudflare auto-manages DNS since zone is theirs
5. Do Task 8 (Search Console + Bing) — can run in parallel with 7
6. After 24-48h: do Task 9 (Lighthouse audit)
